column1: Currently a work in progress. Feedback is welcome.

column3: Will explain what makes a comp different from release, and what has been added or changed.

column1: Type of comp: Remaster, Vanilla, overhaul, renovation, extension, remix, shitpost, and playlist.
Era refers to an album, or period of time that the comp is based on or taking place during.
column2: A comps cover will be displayed in this column. If there is no custom cover, a default cover will be used.
column3: Dry descriptions that act as a guide. Quality can, and will vary without disclosure here. Playlists are denoted as such, typically put together by one individual using others edits with personal touches applied at the discretion of the playlist maker, credits are included when applicable here. Due to the nature of the yedits archive, listings may be updated as new information is uncovered or presented. If you see something you believe is incorrect, let me know and I will try to verify this information before updating it. This archive ideally will be shared physically, and digitally without fear of a DMCA takedown notice being filed someday. If you find this document in an incomplete or incorrect state, you may have an outdated copy, or the work on the archive and/or document here has been abandoned. I hope this spreadsheet is helpful in navigating the archive. Special credit to Sophie for allowing me to borrow color coding from her curated tracker, and some terminology. Thanks to Aiwei for touching up the sheet further. -  100greenmen
column19: Multiple individuals may be listed when applicable
column20: Length of the full comp, not counting bonus tracks
column21: Links to comps will be dropped here, samply links provided by Koji.
column22: Any supplementary material a comp might come with

column3: Kanye West would begin work in the music industry producing beats, and recording with a Chicago based rap group featuring GLC, Timmy G, and Arrowstar while trying to find his sound. Although Kanye's signature sample chopping is present for many of these tracks that were released on mixtapes or leaked, he would later state he was disatisfied with his persona for his verses, and would course correct to a more authentic one going forward.  The era currently only has one comp but future comps would need to be based on the tracks produced with the Go-Getters to fit into this slot.

World Record Holders compiles and remasters the tracks produced by the Go-Getters in the vein of old school hip-hop with Kanye's iconic sample choping on display. Goose's contributions include new production, remixed and/or re-arranged verses, and mixing to make the overall quality of the comp consistent. Goose has stated he would like to remaster/remake this comp himself. -  100greenmen

gv's personal remaster of Goose's  World Record Holders comp, part of a personal timeline adjacent to big brother's earth 2054 timeline. Although the tracklist is almost identical, the track 16. "His Name Means Love (Outro)" was omitted entirely.  - 100greenmen

Kanye West's first studio album released in 2003. After many attempts to find a label that would release his music, this album faced numerous delays and changes in direction (most notably a nearly fatal car crash), utlimately steering away from the usual sounds of hip hop, focusing on a unique style of sampling for the time, lyrics that reflected Kanye's beliefs and anxieties, in the hopes that he could inspire other people with his poetic prose and optimistic outlook on breaking away from established norms. Comps from this era contain a large number of scrapped tracks dating back to Kanye's solo work in the late 90's, and tracks after he signed with Roc-A-Fella, as well as released tracks.

FosterLynch's extensive remaster of college dropout scraps, and other Kanye tracks sourced from various mixtapes released between 1999 and 2002. The Lo-Fi quality is preserved while mixing is updated for modern audio devices. - 100greenmen

FosterLynch's remaster of the college dropout's release tracks, as well as an alternate version of we don't care and a few scrapped tracks. Updated for modern audio devices. May be out of date, however the audio quality and mixing on offer is superior to any given version avaliable on streaming services. - 100greenmen

SimpleJosh's take on a Kanye West Mixtape after he started producing music with Roc-a-fella records, fairly faithful remasters to the tracks from their respective mixtapes. Contains two versions of Drop Dead Gorgeous as bonus tracks, and a few early versions of college dropout songs. - 100greenmen

A short selection of tracks, and a custom intro track made before and during The College Dropout era with cleaned up audio, presumably chosen for their consistent old school hip hop sound. A good companion album to listen to alongside the college dropout itself. - 100greenmen

An extension adding new audio bytes, interviews, and samples as intros and outtros to some songs, while others are identical to the official release. Some scrapped or alt takes were added as additional verses to the extended songs, while still maintaining the official college dropout tracklist. - 100greenmen

A comp that merges skits with songs, extends them with alt/scrapped verses, ad-libs, and adds a few tracks produced during Kanye's time working under Roc-a-fella. Noteably does not change last call. -  100greenmen

column22: Credits list, one bonus track, explanation by gv

column3: Mostly uses edits from SimpleJosh's dream killers, this playlist is gv's personal take on the college dropout adjacent to big brother's earth 2054 timeline. with a fair number of songs from the official tracklist for college dropout and scraps. Meant to be an earlier iteration of what would become the college dropout based on leaks and mixtapes from the time.  -  100greenmen

Slightly tweaked version of the college dropout, some songs come with a mix of minor added production, new mixes, new samples, scrapped verses added back, etc. Some scrapped songs are added back, skits are merged with some songs. Cover uses an ai upscaled photo, likely using an emerging free model from 2022, or possibly earlier.  -  100greenmen
Skits are merged with songs, scrapped verses are added back to some songs, new mixing noteably boosts the vocals. -  100greenmen

The first comp from Xenniversary for his personal timeline. It includes almost every song Kanye made leading up to the College dropout from mixtapes, leaks, etc. Contains some unique transitions, new intros and outtros, and extended samples from the sources. -  100greenmen

A comp aiming to imagine an early version of the college dropout, includes many songs from before Kanye signed with Roc-a-fella, adds a new interlude, new production and mixing emphasizing the bass. -  100greenmen

Kanye West's second studio album, Late Registration released in 2005. After the success of The College Dropout, work on this record would begin with old and new songs as a victory lap to celebrate his breakout success. Numerous delays changed the direction from sounding identical to The College Dropout to a focus on imitating a movie soundtrack. The themes of concious hip-hop continue here as his poetic writing style matures. Late Orchestration was a live performance that included a full orchestra accompanying Kanye's live performances containing songs from Late Registration, and The College Dropout. Comps from this era contain released and scrapped songs worked on during this period, as well as some tracks from the previous.
This comp remasters the audio for the eponymous Late Orchestration Live album. Removes or reduces crowd noise, sounds more like a studio recorded album. -  100greenmen
New instrumental additions to this ep length comp give the tracks a fresh take on the live recordings, with some updated mixing as well.  -  100greenmen

column22: One alt track, credits and explanation by gv

column3: gv's personal take on big brother's earth 2054 timeline version of Late Registration. Utilizing edits from a few different comps of this era, and further editing them. Some songs are carried over from the college dropout era and suited to fit the sound of this particular comp, while others are songs from Common's Album Be. -  100greenmen

Mixing has been adjusted to emphasize the instrumentals, new orchestral production added on some tracks, skits merged with tracks. An edit of bittersweet poetry is included, in the spirit of it's original intended inclusion with the album. -  100greenmen
The Good, The Bad, & The Ugly
column22: Five bonus tracks, alt with fewer added verses/features is avaliable.

column3: An alternative take on the era that depicts a collaboration between Kanye, Common, and Consequence. Songs are taken from a variety of projects from the time Late Registration was released, and adds features from the other artists to the others respective songs despite some quirks with the vocal stems used. Includes new production as well. An alternative version of the comp is avaliable without the additional features and verses. -  100greenmen
A simple comp that adds extended intros and outtros to various songs, also includes the track back to basics. -  100greenmen

A comp with a reduced tracklist completed for Yedits 2026 one-week-comp competittion, it uses some live vocals from Late Orchestration, remastered by Bobert with with the intent of re-creating the CD release of Late Orchestration in a higher quality. Minor production elements are added as well, the alt track Diamonds from Sierra Lione replaces Jay-Z With Lupe Fiasco. - 100greenmen
An extension adding back scrapped/alt verses, skits are merged with songs and some songs are combined as well. Extended intros and outtros. -  100greenmen
FosterLynch's remaster of Late Registration, includes Back to basics and We can make it better. Overall higher quality audio than what most streaming services have on offer. -  100greenmen

Kanye West's third studio album graduation released in 2007. The movie soundtrack inspired hip-hop Kanye Had produced for Late registration is expanded upon in this record ultimately resulting in a love letter to pop culture, that went on to define pop culture for the rest of the decade. Lyrically it focuses on emotional introspection and expression of the ego. Different regions had remixes of some songs as bonus tracks, and The track Bittersweet Poetry was included in the Japanese release of the album as a bonus. The track Good Night was included on streaming services as a bonus. Comps from this era contain the release tracks, tracks he produced for during the late 2000's and a few choice tracks from Late Registration.
An extension of graduation adding back scrapped or alt verses, new instrumental intros and outtros, and extended samples from their source. Also includes bittersweet poetry.  -  100greenmen

A collaborative project aiming to make the definitive Graduation listening experience. Features a variety of tweaks such as new mixing, extended intros and outtros, a few extended songs, new skits, and American boy has been added to the tracklist.  -  100greenmen
A comp as ahmed2.0 has described is similar to late registration in sound. Has some added production, and some scrapped/alt verses. Some tracks remain unaltered.  -  100greenmen

Most songs have had entirely new instrumentals that remain faithful to the oringial, other songs have new production, and a handful of extended intros have been added. New electronic and soul samples are also present.  Maintains the release tracklist.  -  100greenmen
Fosterlynch's remaster of graduation, includes bitter sweet poetry. Higher quality preservation of subtleties that would otherwise be quieter on streaming services.  -  100greenmen

column22: Multiple file formats, early scraps included.

column3: A shorter graduation comp that adds a lot of new production, some instrumentals are re-created and expanded on. Expanded samples and features are also added with new intros on a few songs, along with unique sample chops.  -  100greenmen
A playlist with some edits from the editor who put it together. Lots of added features and scrapped/alt verses. Includes American Boy, and Us Placers in the tracklist. - 100greenmen

A short comp with new instrumentals in the style of The College Dropout, and an altered tracklist to include scrapped tracks, as well as a new intro and outtro, New soul samples and beats. Also includes a Diamonds and Dollars Edit. - 100greenmen

An extension that adds intros and outtros from different versions of the songs from the glow in the dark tour, and some leaks. Lots of new features are added from other artists. The Ne-yo song Because of you is also added with an extended instrumental to make room for a verse featuring Kanye West. - 100greenmen

column22: Multiple alt covers, multiple file formats.

column3: This comp takes scrapped songs from Late Registration, Graudation, and Common's album Be to make an entirely fabricated take on a Kanye album from this time period. Kanye's Track from the 'Voices of a grass roots movement' album produced to promote Barack Obama's 2008 Presidential run is also included. Added production on every track makes for a sound similar to Late Registration with some old-school hip-hop influence and mixing to match. - 100greenmen

Up to Graduation Kanye had a plan to release 4 albums, the last of which was intended to be Good Ass Job. However after Graduation was released his mother had unexpectedly passed away and his then Fiance broke up with him. This series of events lead to such a drastic change in Kanye's life he felt compelled to interrupt work on Good Ass Job to express his grief and lonliness. Work would begin in short and the resulting album Inspired by Kid Cudi's first mixtape (who was also recruited to write for the album) was released in 2008 as his fourth studio album. Some minor experiments with the eponymous 808's Synthisizer from GAJ and his practice with singing were utilized to form the sound on this album alongside heavy useage of auto-tune, all elements that were criticized at the time upon it's release. The orchestral elements from previous eras were still present, although they did little to win over fans and critics who did not like the new sound. This album would go on to inspire many other artists, most noteably Drake, and begin Kanye's trend of re-inventing his sound with every new project. Comps from this era contain in part or whole the tracklist of the released album, with one exception.
A variety of minor changes such as adding and removing verses, using different versions and mixing them with the release tracks, and even using later Live from Hollywood Bowl versions. - 100greenmen

column22: 3 Bonus tracks, and an alt cover.

column3: The instrumentals for this album have been re-created and given more layers of synths and bass, some vocals and orchestral instrumentals taken from Live at Hollywood Bowl.  - 100greenmen

This comp reduces the tracklist and uses leaked tracks with a rougher vocal delivery and lighter autotune. Also adds some additional production and references making for a very different sound while remaining faithful to the work Kanye did on the album. - 100greenmen
New production is added, scrapped/alt features are added alongside kanye's regular verses.  - 100greenmen

Adds a bit of new production to some songs while others have new synths entirely, adds in new features alongside a re-ordered tracklist. Also contains Find Your Love by Drake with an added Kanye feature.  - 100greenmen

Re-creates different productions elements from other versions and performances of the songs off of 808's, most noteably VH1 Storytellers, his second live recording album. Some songs are quite similar to release, while other songs have more drastic changes that still remain faithful. Also features minor mixing differences. - 100greenmen

An original project inspired by 808's with all new instrumentals, tracks, and vocals using early ai masking over the creators own voice. Meant to serve as a sequel with the same core ideas, emotions, and sounds of Kanye's album.  - 100greenmen
An extension of 808's with no additional tracks. Instrumentals are extended and scrapped/alt features are added back. - 100greenmen
This comp adds new production of varying degrees alongside new skits baked into some of the tracks. The tracklist is cut down, and a few songs were delegated to bonus tracks. - 100greenmen

A mashup of various Childish Gambino tracks with 808's. features vocals from Childish Gambino and his collaborators, pulling from released and unreleased material. Some instrumentation is also brought over from his tracks as well, as well as adjusted mixing.  - 100greenmen
808's & Heartbreak remastered for modern audio devices, with removed clipping. - 100greenmen

This comp features a re-done and reordered tracklisting, alongside some new interlude tracks. New production mostly with synths on every song, and some added features to a few tracks. Heartless and Pinnochio story have been combined into a single track. - 100greenmen

Initially formed in 2007, the rap group comprised of Lupe Fiasco, Kanye West, and Pharrell Williams produced it's first single; Us Placers in the same year. The song appeared on Kanye West's Can't Tell Me Nothing mix tape, and generated some excitement for the group's first album. Unfortunately due to busy schedules none of the artists were able to do much for it, and after putting it on hold for an extended period of time, it was unceremoniously cancelled in 2013. Comps for this era feature songs from all three artists, adding features from the other members and some frequent collaborators. A total of 3 songs were ever produced with all 3 members involvement, and a handful of unfinished scraps that have leaked from 2007 to 2010, with the name God Unwilling considered for a potential album release. Although Lupe Fiasco would use the name again for his own solo projects in 2024, this era is unrelated to his work past this time period. While not considered a real era, comps under this umbrella aim to create simulacrums of what a CRS album might have sounded like. Songs and sounds are varied borrowing from each artists production style, and encompasses concious hip-hop and energetic club bangers.

Songs are taken from all three artists discography with features added from each other, and some rearranged song structure. A few iconic songs from the Good Ass Job era are featured here. The released CRS songs are unaltered rips. Supposedly one of the first fleshed out CRS comps.   - 100greenmen

SimpleJosh reconstructs snippets, creates new instrumentals, adds verses, reworks beats and more to make a short but high-quality CRS comp. It not only edits the few released CRS songs, but also uses new samples and known snippets to make a full length comp out of the era while still remaining accurate - aiwei

This comp takes songs and verses up to 2013 from each artist and adds more features from each other. A few samples are extended, borrowed from other songs, or emphasized with slightly altered mixing on the released tracks. - 100greenmen

This scrapped comp has the most unique tracklist of any CRS comp. Many are mashups in a similar vein with unfinished mixing, possible placeholder verses and production. Apparently started and abandoned in 2024, it wasn't found until Chicken Man released it in 2026 with no clear intent to finish it. - 100greenmen

The most coveted, and infamous unreleased album from Kanye West. Previously said to have been the fourth of the college trilogy set to release after Graduation, it experienced it's first delay after Donda West's passing, and again after Kanye's interruption during Taylor Swift's 2009 VMA award acceptance speech. Production began after Graduation, and shifted focus with each delay. Avaliable leaks that aren't just beats feature more melodic singing and some experiments with the 808's synthesizer. A myriad of themes and sounds were covered albiet, many songs are noticeably unfinished missing verses, frequent use of kanye mumble, even songs that have yet to be heard. Many collaborators were brought on for features and production ideas before the project ultimately transitioned into My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy in spring of 2010. Comps of this era may share some crossover with MBDTF as many songs would still be worked on during that period of time. More frequently GAJ is envisioned either before or after the VMA's incident with the former having a more narrow scope, and the latter combining unreleased tracks with earlier versions of released tracks.  There was also an album title "Winter Spring Summer Fall" which would feature songs for every season. Little is known of this particular album as the title would not be used long, some comps here attempt to re-create this idea using songs from this era.

column2: To promote MBDTF's release, Kanye would release songs under the 'GOOD Fridays' moniker every Friday from 8/20/2010 to 12/15/2010. Although the album proper released on 11/22/2010. Some GOOD Fridays tracks have been used in GAJ comps due to their relative completion, as they had been worked on during the time the album was still considered Good Ass Job. A verse from Rhymefest's Raw Dawg mixtape was taken for an edit by Ivy & Casual, made into an intro skit, periodically included in these comps.

column3: A comp with a mix of reworked MBDTF songs and scraps as well as a handful of GAJ songs, reenvisioning the album as a synth-pop influenced experience. Icy's added production is impressive and unique, with its reimagining of songs being especially respectable. Although mixing is questionable at times, it definitely still stands out in its overall creativity and quality. - aiwei

One of the first widespread and noteworthy GAJ comps attempting to overhaul the era. A mix of raw leaks and edited files with new samples and verses to fill empty space or replace mumble. Would also go on to inspire other influential and trivial comps. The outtakes are serviceable on their own, even if they are clearly undercooked. - 100greenmen

Santino's debut comp, and while they would become an extremely influential editor, its age shows in its mixing and audio quality. Despite that, the comp contains a multitude of very creative edits, with added verses, production and features rounding out its tracks. It paved the way for Santino's later work in the era, laying down the standard for Santino's reputation of unique tracklisting, serving as an introduction to Santino's later contributions to the GAJ era as a whole. - aiwei.  Also lowkey delete ALL of my edits yo that shit needs to stay gone. - Santino

Akimbo's comp updates the mixing on the tracks for this comp while adding some additional prod to fit in with the electronic songs for the era. Some songs recieved more attention than others, minor trimming was also applied to songs shortening intros and outtros. Also includes added features, almost no mumble, and an emphasis on tracks with Kanye's singing. - 100greenmen

This early comp mainly uses GOOD Friday tracks and GAJ leaks without mumble from the era, with some shortened songs serving as interludes, and a speech Kanye would give during a live performance titled "Pinnochio story 2". - 100greenmen

column22: A few .m4a's, alt cover, credits, compressed V1 copy.

column3: This comp focuses on re-arranging sections of songs, adding features, and in some cases mashing two versions of songs together. An interlude was cobbled together from Kanye's interruption at the VMA's and a news report about it, with the background chanting from Power. A  V1 of the comp  titled "Album for Will" is archived with the comp, it's mixed horribly, and uses bad extracts. I like to pretend it doesn't exist. - 100greenmen. I also like to pretend V1 doesn’t exist. - Bendaboi

Debut comp from Ghostbuster2k, he has re-arranged many samples, verses, and added his own production to many of the songs here. Mixing has been adjusted to make the songs sound more consistent, and contains some unique twists on skits. Sounds like a realistic release from Kanye from before the VMA's incident. - 100greenmen

column22: Multiple file formats, instrumentals, bonus tracks.

column3: Graybones re-creates several instrumentals with pure electronic synths and adds some to the rest of the tracks. The vocal extracts are outdated, though they are still at a listenable quality. A few features are added and some verse re-arranging has been done. - 100greenmen

Based on the cancelled tour of the same name, a would be collaboration between Kanye West and Lady Gaga. Scrapped Lady Gaga songs from The Fame and GAJ are utilized. Gaga is added to Kanye's Tracks and vice verse, with the exception of the final track. Production in line with Gaga's electro-dance sound is added to the GAJ tracks, with some minor touch ups on The Fame tracks. - 100greenmen

column22: Art, bonus tracks, multiple file formats, credits.

column3: It is no exaggeration to call this the most famous and influential comp of Yedits history. Often considered definitive despite its age it was a landmark for its use of AI to reduce mumble the remade instrumentals among other prominent features. There are a littany of changes to go over, and they did everything possible to make a comp that sounds "finished" building on established conventions to give the songs within a unique identity. While some may consider it outdated by modern standards, its instrumentals and stems were used in other comps going forward, reinforcing the quality work done here. - 100greenmen

column22: Art, bonus tracks, multiple file formats, credits.

column3: This comp has minimal overlap with its Pastelle produced inspiration, instead focusing on more obscure and less edited pre-VMA tracks, giving them previously never-before-seen overhauls. Each song has added production, verses, beatswitches, fleshed out textures, additional sections, and more changes which all contribute to edits that feel like brand-new songs when compared to their contemporaries. It also minimizes its use of Pastelle's GAJ and its edits of popular tracks maintain their personality, with their interpretations being unique amongst the sea of others, such as in the comp's Flashing Lights 2 or Throw Money Everywhere. - aiwei

Biob's first pass at a GAJ comp; mostly re-arranging, mixing different song versions together, and adding minor production elements to mimic what a GAJ comp could have looked like in early 2010.  Existing samples for the project are also applied in a few spots, mixing has been altered to emphasize bass when it is present on a track. Sounds closer to an 'old-kanye' album and would fit in as a natural evolution of his style from 2007. - 100greenmen

A release ready comp, lots of added production is applied to fill out the instrumentals and a tracklist with minimal mumble is utilized. New samples chops and features are also prevelant, making for an interpretation of the GAJ era that feels realistic to Kanye's production style from Graduation. - 100greenmen

Remastered tracks that have added production, albiet lightly applied. Verses are added from other songs but otherwise quite a bit of mumble is deliberately left on for those who prefer the way leaked tracks sound, alongside their GOOD Fridays counter parts. Many older versions of GAJ tracks are used for a stripped back feel. - 100greenmen

Released alongside Simplejosh's Good ass job (V2) comp, this era accurate shitpost pitch shifts the vocals and instrumentals selectively to make a more off-putting and painful listening experience.  - 100greenmen

Likely one of, if not the first, of Mochi&Broccoli's comps. Featured artists verses are swapped with other Kanye verses and some remastering is done, with varying results. Some unique mashups help the comp stand out from others, but otherwise it's very indicitave of comp creation from the early 2020's.  - 100greenmen

High quality remade and touched up instrumentals focusing on live instruments with minimal retained synth production. Some samples are also used less frequently or removed altogether, and features are added to some songs. Feels intended to sound like what My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy would on release. - 100greenmen
A playlist from the early 2020's, made with a mix of edits and leaks, as well as R1600's custom edits. - 100greenmen

column22: 1 bonus track, 3 scraps, art, and instrumentals

column3: JuyTuy's first Gaj comp, many instrumentals have had a lot of small samples or new production at points to create a fuller sound for each song while remaining faithful. Also features an impressive ai de-mumble of the track See me again. Overall a realistic sound for a 2009 release of the album. - 100greenmen

Frickinfire's first GAJ playlist, this one seperates the tracks into two discs. No particular theme or reason is discernable other than splitting the large tracklist down for easier listening. A good way to experience a near complete list of the most GAJ edits for earlier versions of songs when fewer leaks had been discovered, with some mumble. Also includes the track Kinda like a big deal from Clipse. - 100greenmen

column22: 5 bonus tracks, 1 alt track, unknown credits.

column3: Frickinfire's second GAJ playlist reduced the overall tracklist. The same edits from the same tracks as the previous iteration remain. - 100greenmen

column22: 5 bonus tracks, 1 alt track, unknown credits.

column3: Based on Simplejosh's V2 comp, this re-structures songs and uses alternative edits likely sourced from /r/yedits to make a personalized version of the comp.  More of a novelty, but it does contain unique, uncredited edits. - 100greenmen

A series of playlists made with various edits and some custom edits from Sophie. As she described it herself "I is pre-vmas, II is main post vmas, III is wssf made using og mbdtf stuff, IV is II deluxe aka extra tracks, V is an entirely alts and bonus versions of songs" The gargantuan length is broken up into 5 parts and organized accordingly. Almost every song from the era known is covered up to Febuary 2026.   - 100greenmen

V1 of this comp has a selection of songs from the era with an added interlude, and Don't stop from CRS. Some tracks have added verses, mixing is inconsistent. V2 includes verses on a few songs that didn't have finished vocals, and an altered tracklist.  - 100greenmen

Based on a tracklist by Big Brother for his alt-universe, the intent is to make a hypothetical album after Good Ass Job based on the early versions of MBDTF tracks and GOOD Fridays tracks. Minimalistic changes that overall alter the mood and atmosphere from the edits included. Production is consistently lo-fi for the edits chosen.   - 100greenmen

A hypothetical collaborative album between Kanye West and MF Doom using songs from around the time. Production is added matching MF Doom's usual sound and features are added from each artist to the others songs.  - 100greenmen

Simplejosh's more experimental take on the era. Includes some mashups and many alt verses/features for tracks, and some added production that stands out from his main comp. The directors cut adds more songs, noteably armed and dangerous. - 100greenmen
A short GAJ era rework comp that focuses on the more pop-rap side of the era and the upbeat and unheard side of 808s, while reworking the songs with added synth production. - Ace

column22: Multiple alt covers, multiple file formats.

column3: JuyTuy's second GAJ comp. It pulls from a handful of songs from the era by Kanye's frequent collaborators including Drake's show me a good time, a song rumored to have been worked on by Kanye. Added synths, drum beats, and verses have helped make it a realistic ep length project. Also features passenger/buildings from the Graduation era split into two songs. - 100greenmen

column22: 2 Alt covers, 6 alt tracks, 6 bonus tracks.

column3: Biob's second GAJ comp, this time based on a beat-tape that leaked with the title "Good Ass Job beats for Kanye". Verses and features are added to the few beats with no known vocals recorded and other songs with less on them. Some added samples and production are present, as a form of polish. - 100greenmen

New beat chops, samples, punch-ins, and remade beats for each track with no mumble. This comp was made for the 2026 Yedits one-week-comp contest, the ep length tracklist uses some popular GAJ songs and gives them a level of production to where these could be considered release-ready. - 100greenmen
Uses early versions of songs with tracks that would be worked on late into the era, and GOOD Fridays tracks. Features and synths are added with updated mixing to maintain consistency. - 100greenmen

A playlist pulling from various editors and including a few leaks. Demumbles and upscaled tracks make for a higher quality and more consistent listening experience. The main cover is an ai upscale of an existing cover usually used for the era. - 100greenmen

An early example of a comp trying to make an alternative take on the era from 2022/2023. Combining different song versions, with some minor production elements and verses/features shifted around or added in addition to a few songs from the 808's & HB era. Somewhat outdated mixing and quite a bit of mumble left in. - 100greenmen

A combination of re-created beats and vanilla style touch ups make up an alternative take on the era that still feels familiar. A few songs from previous eras are present and made to fit in quite well. Added features and verses also help fill in otherwise empty or repetitive tracks. - 100greenmen

A comp that borrows songs of the era from some of Kanye's frequent collaborators. New mixing emphasizing bass and added synth production make for a basic comp, letting the track order fill out the vague theme of the comps namesake. - 100greenmen

Intended as a B-Side to Santino's first GAJ comp. Having been produced some time after, his edits on WSSF are more polished and high quality. It has a very unique song selection, editing some relatively obscure and some non-Kanye songs. It reworks these songs with a massive amount of added features and nice production changes to make each track feel fresh. - aiwei

Split up into four 3-track discs, with each one representing the corresponding season per the title of the comp. New synth production and re-created beats with added beats make up the bulk of what's on offer. Some songs from Kanye's collaborators are inlcuded as well. - 100greenmen

Mostly featuring GOOD Fridays songs working as sequel to BSB Dante's earlier GAJ comp. Uses the latest avaliable release of any given song as a base, with added/alt verses from other versions. A few songs seem to be unaltered. - 100greenmen

This comp uses early versions of MBDTF tracks and GOOD Fridays tracks and extends them with scrapped/alt verses and features, as well as sparce new production, split into 4 discs for an easily digestible listening experience each with their own short narrative order.A few songs are left unedited. - 100greenmen

A comp using edits and leaks, building on them further to personalize the comp. Mostly made up of added verses and combining different versions of songs. There is some ai vocal useage, custom samples and samplechops, and some added production used sparingly. Uses tracks across the era for a good look at what a finished GAJ could have been. - 100greenmen

A very early GAJ era comp adding more intense autotune, verses, and some new production. It's likely a serious attempt at making a comp but the quality of autotune, and mumble verses used is questionable. - 100greenmen

After a lengthy and expensive production cycle MBDTF was finally released in November of 2010. The enormous number of songs that had been worked on since 2008 had been trimmed down to a more concise selection, with a myriad of features and artists co-producing or lending their own talents to elements of the album. The album ultimately contained a mixture of Hip-hop production techniques, synthetic and real orchestral instruments, and several iconic samples. Multiple album covers were painted by George Condo, the most familiar of which was censored for public release. The intention of making an apology album and wanting to be accepted again after dealing with suicidal ideation and depression in the aftermath of the VMA's incident was clearly felt. He expressed how he felt as a celebrity, his views on society and the industry, as well as a strong desire to continue living the celebrity life despite the darker side of that lifestyle. Often hailed as his magnum opus, it was well recieved at the time and to this day remains a unique cultural landmark of Hip-Hop and Pop music. Comps from this era will mostly feature tracks that were on the released tracklist as well as GOOD Fridays releases. Other songs from the era and GAJ are not excluded, but typically will be used for a GAJ comp instead. Some ochestral re-creations of life performances of MBDTF with more instruments are also included in aid of the unique soundscape of the album.

column2: Although Big Brother, Formerly known as Themusicnerdguy has requested I purge his older works from my archive, I refuse on the basis it's work he does not legally own, and he cannot ask such a thing.

column3: A fabrication of a Hypothetical 10th Anniversary for MBDTF, remastered with updated mixing and included in two additional discs are some tracks under the Child Rebel Soldier name, and demos, early versions of some MBDTF songs. - 100greenmen

An extension combining multiple songs, extending intros and outtros with the source of the samples used. Alt/scrapped verses added, Devil in a new dress had the track White Dress added to it. - 100greenmen
Apart from slightly louder audio, the audio clipping has been eliminated entirely, with subtle details restored. - 100greenmen
My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy Deluxe

Tracks for this comp are based on a proposed itunes tracklisting for a deluxe edition of MBDTF, with a few liberties taken. New production, added verses, and mixing very similar to release MBDTF make for a good companion piece to the album proper. - 100greenmen
My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy Demos

An early example of a comp, the tracks here are the earliest iterations of MBDTF's tracks during 2020. Compiled in the same order as release, with the only absentee's being the All of the lights interlude and Who will survive in America. - 100greenmen
MY BEAUTIFUL DARK TWISTED FREQUENCY REDUX  THE GOOD ASS FANTASY
A comp combining songs and verses from the GAJ era to MBDTF. Likely intended to be tedious to sit thorugh, including almost every verse for every song. - 100greenmen
MY BEAUTIFUL DARK TWISTED FANTASY (Mike Dean inspired)
Meant to emulate Mike Dean's production style, synth strings are added to the tracks adding more depth, without altering the tone too much. Mixing was also adjusted to accomodate. - 100greenmen

A tracklist taking tracks from GOOD Fridays and MBDTF with the inclusion of Never see me again. Intended to be a realistic completed GAJ with these tracks. Has added production focused on drum beats and bass, with new and repurposed samples. - 100greenmen
This is a de-tagged copy of the Record4Hype Zip containing what was likely an early copy of MBDTF sent to Hype Williams for review. - 100greenmen

This extension adds a lot of alt/scrapped verses and takes, longer samples from the source and combining a few songs. Based on the Record4Hype tracks while also including some GOOD Fridays tracks. - 100greenmen
This is a Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy

Alongside some added custom skits and extended intro and outtro samples, mixing is altered to emphasize bass and some GOOD Friday tracks are added alongside the release tracks. The tracklist is also very different from the usual suspects. Some songs also have additional vocal effects and production, and alternate vocal takes. - 100greenmen

A short, curated selection of tracks from MBDTF with added orchestral production. Based on earlier releases of Kanye albums with similar ideas (i;e Late Orchestration) it's an alternative take on what could have been if Kanye had taken a different direction with MBDTF entirely. - 100greenmen
